Details: Ion exchange is a best available technology for removal of cyanide [1320]. In bench-scale tests using packed bed anion exchange columns, initial removal of cyanide was 99.9 percent from a starting concentration of 200 mg/L. This removal was maintained for 80 to 90 bed volumes. The columns were completely exhausted at 250 to 420 bed volumes, depending on pH and the presence or absence of zinc [2459].
